title: Teriyaki Chicken
tagline: Homemade teriyaki sauce beats bottled every time. Sweet, salty, and glossy.
description: Chicken thighs glazed in a sticky homemade teriyaki sauce. Serve over rice with steamed vegetables.
ingredients: 1/4 cup mirin (or 3 Tbsp rice vinegar + 1 Tbsp sugar)
Sesame seeds and green onions for garnish
steps: Whisk together soy sauce, mirin, sake, brown sugar, garlic, and ginger in a bowl. Set aside.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken thighs and cook 4-5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through (165°F). Remove to a plate.
Pour teriyaki sauce into the skillet. Bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits.
Mix cornstarch with water. Stir into the sauce and cook 1-2 minutes until thickened.
Return chicken to the skillet. Coat in sauce and cook 1-2 minutes, flipping to glaze both sides.
Slice chicken and serve over rice. Drizzle with extra sauce, sesame seeds, and green onions.
notes: Real teriyaki: The word means 'glossy grilled.' The sauce should be sticky and shiny.
Mirin: This sweet rice wine is key. Find it in the Asian aisle. Don't skip it.
No sake: Dry sherry or white wine work. Or use more mirin.
Grilled version: Grill chicken and brush with sauce during the last few minutes.
Chicken breast: Works but thighs are juicier and more forgiving.
